completion_summary: All 27 New Testament books have complete first-pass source-audited draft restored translations and aligned mystical companion layers. Verse-number alignment is preserved. Each book has a progress tracker with source-witness audit flags and interpretive safeguards.
collection_translation_safeguards: Use earliest available source witnesses as the audit target: Greek New Testament textual traditions for NT books, Septuagint/citation texture where NT authors quote Greek Jewish Scriptures, and Hebrew/Aramaic source traditions for Tanakh/Old Testament continuation.
Restored/source-audited layer must remain distinct from mystical companion layer.
Mystical companion layer is interpretive and contemplative, never a replacement for source-audited restored text.
Verse-number alignment must remain intact across restored and mystical files.
Avoid anti-Jewish or supersessionist readings. Preserve Jewish context, Scripture continuity, covenant complexity, and intra-Jewish debate where present.
Do not use apocalyptic, demonological, or adversarial language to target people or modern groups.
For household, slavery, women, leadership, and submission texts, preserve source wording while adding anti-abuse, anti-domination, and human-dignity safeguards.
For wealth, empire, and economics texts, distinguish survival needs from exploitative luxury, coercion, greed, and commodification of human lives.
For warning and judgment texts, preserve severity while preventing fear-coercion and dehumanization.
For embodiment texts, avoid body-hatred; flesh/sarx is contextual and must not be collapsed into contempt for bodies.
next_phase: Continue common scaffold Old Testament / Tanakh-first-source work. Suggested next book: Exodus.
next_phase_notes: Exodus should be handled as liberation, covenant, name revelation, Passover, wilderness formation, law, tabernacle, presence, and anti-oppression source text.
Use Hebrew source terms where relevant: YHWH/Tetragrammaton, Elohim, ruach, berit, avad/avodah, pesach, mishkan, kavod, chesed where applicable.
Preserve Egyptian imperial/oppression context without demonizing Egyptians as an ethnicity.
Pharaoh hardening language requires careful source and theological handling.
Law/covenant material should not be framed as legalistic burden but as liberation-shaped covenant instruction.
Tabernacle/presence material should connect to later Hebrews/Revelation only after preserving Exodus on its own terms.
